item.title: Building software with slurm jobs using EasyBuild
item.author: Mikael Öhman
item.description:
Building software with slurm jobs using EasyBuild

This blogpost serves as inspiration and get you started running jobs based on some of the stuff I run on my clusters.

Do you want to conveniently build modules in a hurry? Then you will love --job combined with -r.

item.title: EUM'26 - Day 3
item.author: Kenneth Hoste
item.description:
EUM'26 - Day 3

On day 3 of the 11th EasyBuild User Meeting (EUM'26), we shifted our focus a bit towards EESSI, and also featured a guest keynote speaker from Fujitsu. Attendees actively engaged in discussions, which made it more challenging to stick to the schedule.

This blog post covers the third day of the 3-day event, see also day 1 and day 2.

item.title: EUM'26 - Day 2
item.author: Kenneth Hoste
item.description:
EUM'26 - Day 2

On the second day of the 11th EasyBuild User Meeting (EUM'26), we had a busy agenda consisting of more EasyBuild site talks, a discussion on the use of LLMs, presentations focusing on recent developments in EasyBuild, and looking over the fence to other projects: Spack, Slinky, EPICURE, Lmod, and flux.

This blog post covers the second day of the 3-day event, see also day 1 and day 3.

item.title: EUM'26 - Day 1
item.author: Kenneth Hoste
item.description:
EUM'26 - Day 1

The 11th EasyBuild User Meeting (EUM'26) took place in the beautiful city of Guimarães, the home of the EuroHPC supercomputer Deucalion.

The EasyBuild community (and beyond) gathered again to learn more about recent developments, letting HPC sites that use EasyBuild showcase how they are doing so, attend presentations on projects (loosely) related to EasyBuild, and discuss the future of EasyBuild, including the (potential) impact of LLMs.

The event was sponsored by Centro Nacional de Computação Avançada (the Portuguese National Center for Advanced Computing), INESC TEC, Fujitsu, Do IT Now, Inuits, Microsoft Azure, and NVIDIA.

This blog post covers the first day of the 3-day event, see also day 2 and day 3.

item.title: Interactive Shell to Debug Failed Builds
item.author: Simon Branford
item.description:
Interactive Shell to Debug Failed Builds

One of the big enhancements in EasyBuild v5 is the support for starting an interactive shell to debug failed builds by simply running the auto-generated cmd.sh script.

We would all prefer that no installation ever fails, but there will be times when they do.

The new interactive shell feature provides a way to explore the build environment and debug the failure.

item.title: EasyBuild v5.1.0
item.author: Kenneth Hoste
item.description:
EasyBuild v5.1.0

EasyBuild v5.1.0 was released on 26 May 2025.

This version includes a couple of changes and enhancements we want to briefly highlight in this blog post.

For a detailed overview of all changes included in EasyBuild v5.1.0, see the release notes.

item.title: 10th EasyBuild User Meeting (EUM'25)
item.author: Helena Vela Beltran
Kenneth Hoste
item.description:
10th EasyBuild User Meeting (EUM'25)

This first blog post reports on the 10th EasyBuild User Meeting (EUM'25), which took place 25-27 March 2025 at the Jülich Supercomputing Centre (JSC) in Germany.

Slides of all talks are available here, recordings via YouTube.
